channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: You closed the case, but it was not fixed at all, I took photos today at 4.30 pm, 20th April
item.description:
I reported this area next to Mountview and a bus stop C yesterday( Sunday) as in urgent need of through clean and found on app ' case closed' but as I passed there today it was not only not fixed but even worse. Please look at photos. Please make sure it is done before closing the case.Also, sweeping must be eccelarated in this area and more proper bins and anti letter signs installed, please.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 120, Peckham Hill Street, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Continued Issues with Neighbours Parking Intrusively, 20th April
item.description:
The newly moved in neighbours have consistently been parking multiple different cars in the council garden in front of their house by driving through everyone else’s front gardens ever since moving in. The location of the issue is the gardens along 1549 to 1555 Great Cambridge Road directly in front of local houses are the gardens mentioned. The neighbours of 1555 have also been threatening reports and complaints against the other residents as they come home at their front doors due to supposed parking issues despite their being available parking spaces further up the street or on the Main Street leading to the front of the houses. This has consistently been damaging the gardens of everyone else on the line. Up until this point the entire row of houses was in good terms and the parking arrangements worked for everyone including the previous residents of 1555 despite the previous owners having as many cars as the new owners.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Great Cambridge Road, Enfield
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Unwanted Tyre and Car Part Dumped on Pavement, 20th April
item.description:
A large unwanted tyre and broken car part has been left dumped on the pavement. Can the local team please remove them. Thank you.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Waverley Road, Plumstead

item.title: Dangerous tree, 20th April
item.description:
Dear Sir/Madam, I hope you are well. I am writing to raise a health and safety concern regarding a tree at 51 Marden Crescent DA5 , and to kindly request the council’s assistance. Some large branches have already fallen from the tree, and we have had verbal confirmation from a tree surgeon that it may be dead and potentially unsafe. This is particularly worrying as there are children living at and visiting the property. Please note that the photographs provided only show some of the smaller branches that have fallen. Unfortunately, we do not have photos of the larger branches, but these have also come down and are of greater concern. We have previously reported this issue to the letting agency Anthony Martin Bexleyheath responsible for the property, but unfortunately no action has been taken so far. As a result, we felt it would be best to contact the council for guidance and support. We would be very grateful if this could be looked into, and if an inspection could be arranged at your earliest convenience. Thank you very much for your time and assistance.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hall Place Crescent, Crayford, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Trees shedding white fluff that accumulates, 20th April
item.description:
Trees shedding white fluff that accumulates, floats around and gets everywhere. Kerbside and drains are covered with it, is a cause of higher pollen count. The trees themselves are snapping and falling over in high winds, blocking the pedestrian walkway.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Western Way, Thamesmead
Report on FixMyStreet
